topIssues: Frames 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 10: The top line of the 'Room trace' log console is vertically clipped/cut off, showing only the bottom half of the text characters due to a scroll container padding or height calculation error.

Frames 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12: The 'STATUS' column displays 'COMPLETE ..' with trailing dots, indicating a layout truncation issue where the column width is too narrow for the badge or text content.
Frames 2-12: The 'Room trace' logs contain extremely long, raw variable strings (e.g., 'rc_anthropic__status, rc_anthropic__summary...') that are highly repetitive and difficult to read at this size.
fixSuggestions: Adjust the CSS padding/margin or scroll-margin on the 'Room trace' log container to prevent the top line of text from being clipped during active scrolling.
Increase the width of the 'STATUS' column or adjust the padding of the 'COMPLETE' badge to eliminate the ugly '..' truncation artifact.

Simplify the log output in the Room trace for the demo; instead of printing raw database keys, map them to user-friendly labels (e.g., 'Locked Anthropic research fields' instead of listing 10 raw variable names).
